```python
class Solution:
    def validSequence(self, word1: str, word2: str) -> List[int]:
        seq = []
        diff_count = 0
        for i in range(len(word2)):
            if word1[i] != word2[i]:
                diff_count += 1
                seq.append(i)
            if diff_count > 1:
                return []
        return seq
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates through the characters of word2, comparing them to word1. If a mismatch is found, the index is added to the sequence and a counter is incremented. If more than one mismatch is encountered, an empty array is returned. Otherwise, the sequence is returned. This approach is efficient as it has a linear time complexity and space complexity proportional to the length of word2.
